summary: Ukrainian sources report UAV attacks on both sides of the Crimean bridge and power outages across Crimea, while Russian forces shelled Sloviansk causing civilian injuries. Multiple reactive UAVs were observed moving from various Ukrainian regions toward enemy positions, including attacks on Zaporizhzhia fuel stations. Russian air defenses claimed to have downed 168 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian territory and Black Sea areas.
key_points: Ukrainian UAVs struck targets on both sides of the Crimean bridge (Source: @UkraineNow/82103).
Russian forces shelled Sloviansk with multiple launch rocket systems, damaging multi‑storey buildings and injuring three civilians (Source: @UkraineNow/82096).
Crimean electricity networks suffered partial outages in north‑west, central and south‑coastal zones after UAV attacks on infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/82097).
Fuel sales were completely halted in occupied Crimea, with gasoline available only for state services (Source: @UkraineNow/82088).
Russian air defence reported shooting down 168 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian regions and Black/Azov seas (Source: @tass_agency/381737).
Reactive UAVs were observed moving from Mykolaiv region toward Kirovohrad, and from Sumy toward Trostianets (Source: @eRadarrua/97667, @kpszsu/65922).
Two reactive Shahed drones approached Kyiv/Vyshhorod from the north (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67978).
Ukrainian forces reported UAV attacks on Zaporizhzhia fuel station, causing one injury (Source: @UkraineNow/82093).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian UAVs have struck both sides of the Crimean bridge, indicating heightened aerial activity in the region. Russian shelling of Sloviansk caused civilian casualties and damage to residential structures. Power outages and a complete halt to fuel sales in occupied Crimea reflect growing pressure on Russian‑controlled infrastructure.

points: Ukrainian reactive UAVs launched from Mykolaiv region headed toward Kirovohrad, demonstrating cross‑regional strike capability (Source: @eRadarrua/97667).
A group of UAVs from Kursk oblast moved northward toward Sumy, targeting Shostka (Source: @kpszsu/65957).
Multiple UAVs were reported operating from the Black Sea heading south into Odesa region (Source: @kpszsu/65954).
Ukrainian forces reported UAV attacks on Zaporizhzhia fuel station, resulting in one civilian injury (Source: @UkraineNow/82093).
Reactive Shahed drones were observed approaching Kyiv and Vyshhorod from the north (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67978).
Russian forces shelled Sloviansk with multiple launch rocket systems, damaging multi‑storey buildings and wounding two women (30 and 89) and a 31‑year‑old man (Source: @UkraineNow/82096).
Russian air defence claimed to have downed 168 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian regions and Black/Azov seas (Source: @tass_agency/381737).
Ukrainian UAVs attacked both sides of the Crimean bridge, indicating vulnerability of the crossing (Source: @UkraineNow/82103).
Russian tactical aviation activity was noted on the north‑east and south‑east fronts, posing a threat to frontline areas (Source: @kpszsu/65882).
Russian‑controlled Crimean energy company reported partial blackouts in several energy zones after UAV strikes (Source: @UkraineNow/82097).
UAVs from Sumy region moved toward Shostka, indicating pressure on northern Sumy oblast (Source: @kpszsu/65932).
UAVs observed from Kharkiv heading northward toward Bogoduhiv and Chuhuiv (Source: @kpszsu/65928).
UAVs from Kharkiv region also reported heading toward the north‑east of the oblast (Source: @kpszsu/65953).
UAV activity from the Black Sea moving south into Odesa region suggests eastern maritime pressure (Source: @kpszsu/65954).
Russian air defence reported intercepts of Ukrainian UAVs over eastern Russian territories (Source: @tass_agency/381737).
UAVs launched from Kursk oblast moved northward toward Sumy, targeting Shostka (Source: @kpszsu/65957).
Reactive Shahed drones approached Kyiv/Vyshhorod from the north (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67978).
UAVs reported from Mykolaiv region heading toward Kirovohrad, crossing central Ukraine (Source: @eRadarrua/97667).
Ukrainian radar noted MiG‑31K launch carrying X‑47M2 Kinzhal missile from Savaslyka airfield (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67964).
Russian tactical aviation activity noted on north‑east direction, increasing threat to frontline zones (Source: @kpszsu/65882).
UAV attacks on Zaporizhzhia fuel station caused one injury, highlighting southern supply line vulnerability (Source: @UkraineNow/82093).
UAVs from the Black Sea moved southward into Odesa region (Source: @kpszsu/65954).
Ukrainian forces reported UAV strikes in the Dnipropetrovsk area, including Chortomlyk/Nikopol (Source: @eRadarrua/97671).
Russian air defence claimed downing UAVs over the Black Sea, indicating contested southern airspace (Source: @tass_agency/381737).
Power outages reported in Crimea's south‑coastal energy zone after UAV attacks (Source: @UkraineNow/82097).
Russian air defence reported shooting down 168 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian territory and Black/Azov seas (Source: @tass_agency/381737).
Ukrainian reactive Shahed drones approached Kyiv from the north, prompting air defence alerts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67978).
MiG‑31K launch from Savaslyka airfield carrying Kinzhal missile was noted, indicating high‑speed strike capability (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67964).
Ukrainian UAVs conducted cross‑border strikes on the Crimean bridge, showing reach into occupied territory (Source: @UkraineNow/82103).
Ukrainian air alerts were issued in Kyiv at 12:18 and cleared at 12:40, reflecting ongoing aerial threats (Source: @eRadarrua/97643, @eRadarrua/97653).

key_developments: Ukrainian UAVs struck both sides of the Crimean bridge, indicating increased aerial pressure on occupied Crimea (Source: @UkraineNow/82103).
Russian shelling of Sloviansk with multiple launch rocket systems damaged residential buildings and injured three civilians (Source: @UkraineNow/82096).
Crimean electricity networks experienced partial blackouts in multiple zones after UAV attacks on infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/82097).
Fuel sales were completely halted in occupied Crimea, with gasoline limited to state services (Source: @UkraineNow/82088).
Russian air defence claimed to have downed 168 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian regions and Black/Azov seas (Source: @tass_agency/381737).
Reactive Shahed drones approached Kyiv/Vyshhorod from the north, prompting air defence alerts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67978).
Ukrainian UAV attacks on Zaporizhzhia fuel station caused one civilian injury, highlighting southern supply line vulnerability (Source: @UkraineNow/82093).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian UAV operations are extending into occupied Crimea and across multiple fronts, challenging Russian control of critical infrastructure such as the Crimean bridge and energy networks. Russian artillery strikes, exemplified by the Sloviansk attack, continue to pose a direct threat to civilian populations. Russian air defence claims high interception rates, yet the persistence of Ukrainian aerial incursions suggests a contested airspace with ongoing escalation.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian authorities are issuing frequent air alerts in Kyiv and mobilising air defence assets against incoming Shahed drones. Russian‑controlled Crimean utilities are reporting outages and restricting fuel sales, indicating a strain on occupied resources. Both sides appear to be reinforcing front‑line UAV deployments to sustain pressure on each other's logistical nodes.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV flight paths reveals coordinated launches from Mykolaiv, Kursk, and Black Sea origins targeting strategic locations in central and southern Ukraine. The reported MiG‑31K launch with a Kinzhal missile underscores Russia's reliance on high‑speed strike platforms. Continued Russian artillery use of multiple launch rocket systems in urban areas raises the risk of civilian casualties and infrastructure damage.
